unit.note: full client Vitest suite (191 files); server unchanged (client-only terminal input filter)

e2e.note: terminal-rightclick-mouse.spec.ts (real right-click in mouse mode not forwarded; left-click is)

surface_verification.runner: playwright (chromium) vs isolated single-process Hono serving the built SPA + API (temp USERPROFILE, 127.0.0.1:3947)
surface_verification.justification: Real-browser proof against a live pty: with xterm put into SGR mouse-reporting mode (ESC[?1006h ESC[?1000h, what Claude enables), a REAL right-click on the canvas produces NO right-button (<2) report in the sent WS frames — dropped by the onData filter — while a real left-click DOES send a left-button (<0) report. Ran green (1/1). This confirms the fix removes the second (Claude) paste while leaving left/middle/wheel forwarding (Claude selection/scroll) intact.

behaviors.behavior: AC-1: right-button SGR mouse reports (press/release/drag) are NOT forwarded to the pty, so Claude never sees the right-click (no second paste)
behaviors.evidence: terminal-mouse-report.test.ts (Cb=2/34/6 -> true) + EmbeddedTerminal.test.tsx 'drops right-button ...' (onData with <2 not in ws.sent) + e2e terminal-rightclick-mouse.spec.ts (no <2 in sent frames) green

behaviors.behavior: AC-2: left/middle button + wheel reports ARE still forwarded (Claude selection / clicks / scroll unaffected)
behaviors.evidence: terminal-mouse-report.test.ts (Cb=0/1/32/64/65/66 -> false) + EmbeddedTerminal.test.tsx (<0 and <64 present in ws.sent) + e2e (left-click <0 present in sent frames)

behaviors.behavior: AC-3: the classifier matches only complete SGR right-button reports — not keyboard / bracketed-paste / Ctrl+C / legacy-X10 / embedded reports
behaviors.evidence: terminal-mouse-report.test.ts ('plain keyboard/paste/control data' + 'legacy X10' + 'embedded in a larger string' -> false)
